readme.text:
# JSignPdf

[![CI](https://github.com/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf/actions/workflows/pr-builder.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf/actions/workflows/pr-builder.yaml)
[![GitHub release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf?include_prereleases&sort=semver)](https://github.com/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf/releases)
[![SourceForge downloads](https://img.shields.io/sourceforge/dm/jsignpdf.svg)](https://sourceforge.net/projects/jsignpdf/files/latest/download)
[![Translation status](https://hosted.weblate.org/widget/jsignpdf/messages/svg-badge.svg)](https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/jsignpdf/messages/)
[![License: MPL 2.0 / LGPL 2.1](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MPL%202.0%20%2F%20LGPL%202.1-blue)](License.md)

JSignPdf is a Java desktop application for adding digital signatures to PDF documents.
It supports both software keystores (PKCS#12) and hardware tokens / smartcards (PKCS#11),
RFC 3161 timestamping (TSA), OCSP and CRL embedding for long-term validation (LTV),
drag-to-place visible signatures, and a scriptable command-line / batch mode.

Project home page: [intoolswetrust.github.io/jsignpdf](https://intoolswetrust.github.io/jsignpdf/)

![JSignPdf JavaFX UI](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf/master/website/static/img/screenshots/jsignpdf-javafx-main.png)

## Features

- **JavaFX desktop UI** — PDF preview, drag-to-place visible signatures, zoom / page navigation, drag-and-drop document loading, collapsible options panel. Legacy Swing UI remains available via `-Djsignpdf.swing=true`.
- **Command-line / batch mode** — sign many PDFs non-interactively; full CLI parity with the GUI.
- **Keystores**: PKCS#12, Java keystore, and PKCS#11 (hardware tokens, smartcards, HSMs via `conf/pkcs11.cfg`).
- **Timestamping**: RFC 3161 TSA with optional user/password authentication.
- **Revocation info**: CRL and OCSP embedding for LTV workflows.
- **Visible signatures**: customizable layout with `${signer}`, `${timestamp}`, and other placeholders.
- **Internationalization**: maintained via [Weblate](https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/jsignpdf/messages/) (15+ languages).

## Install

Requires a **Java 21 (or newer) JRE** unless you use an installer that bundles its own runtime.

| Platform | Artifact | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Windows** | EXE / MSI / portable ZIP from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf/releases) | Built with `jpackage`, ships a bundled Java 21 runtime. 64-bit only. |
| **Linux** | Flatpak (Flathub submission in progress — tracked in [#307](https://github.com/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf/issues/307)) or cross-platform ZIP | Flatpak bundles its own Java runtime. |
| **macOS** | Cross-platform ZIP | Requires Java 21 on `PATH`. |
| **Any OS with Java 21** | `jsignpdf-<version>.zip` | Run `JSignPdf.sh` / `JSignPdf.bat` from the extracted folder. |
| **Mirror** | [SourceForge](https://sourceforge.net/projects/jsignpdf/files/latest/download) | Same artifacts as GitHub Releases. |

Maven-Central-published artifacts (for embedding the signing engine in your own project) live under `com.github.kwart.jsign`.

## Documentation

- User guide: <https://intoolswetrust.github.io/jsignpdf/>
- Release notes: [`distribution/doc/release-notes/`](distribution/doc/release-notes/)
- Developer / architecture guide: [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)
- Design notes: [`design-doc/`](design-doc/)
- Issue tracker: <https://github.com/intoolswetrust/jsignpdf/issues>

## Translations

Help translate JSignPdf on Weblate: <https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/jsignpdf/messages/>

## Build from source

Requires **Java 21** and Apache Maven.

```bash
mvn clean install
```

The resulting artifacts are produced under `distribution/target/`. See [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for module layout, source-tree overview, and test commands.

### Windows installers

Windows installers are built with `jpackage` as part of the release workflow
(`.github/workflows/do-release.yml`) and produce three artifacts:

- `JSignPdf-<version>.exe` — EXE installer with bundled Java 21 runtime
- `JSignPdf-<version>.msi` — MSI installer with bundled Java 21 runtime
- `JSignPdf-<version>-win-x64.zip` — portable ZIP with bundled Java 21 runtime

For a local experiment on Linux, cross-building requires a Windows JDK + WiX;
the supported path is to let the release workflow build the installers.

### Flatpak

Flatpak packaging lives under [`distribution/linux/flatpak/`](distribution/linux/flatpak/).
Maven dependencies for the Flatpak build are captured in `maven-dependencies.json`
and regenerated by `generate-dependencies.sh` (run by the
`refresh-flatpak-deps.yml` workflow).

## Release process

Releases are workflow-driven via [`.github/workflows/do-release.yml`](.github/workflows/do-release.yml):

1. Add release notes for the new version as
   `distribution/doc/release-notes/<version>.md`. The release workflow uses
   this file as both the bundled README and the GitHub Release body.
2. Trigger the `do-release` workflow manually, supplying the target version.
3. SNAPSHOT builds are published to Maven Central on every push to `master`
   via [`push-snapshots.yaml`](.github/workflows/push-snapshots.yaml).

## License

Dual-licensed under **MPL 2.0** and **LGPL 2.1** — see [License.md](License.md).

---

## Appendix: Testing PKCS#11 without a card reader

A minimal way to exercise the PKCS#11 code path without real hardware is to
back `SunPKCS11` with a software NSS database.

> **Note:** this only works with the `PKCS11` keystore type. `JSignPKCS11`
> does not support NSS keystores.

```bash
echo "pass123+" > /tmp/newpass.txt
echo "dsadasdasdasdadasdasdasdasdsadfwerwerjfdksdjfksdlfhjsdk" > /tmp/noise.txt
mkdir /tmp/nssdb

MODUTIL_CMD="modutil -force -dbdir /tmp/nssdb"
$MODUTIL_CMD -create
$MODUTIL_CMD -changepw "NSS Certificate DB" -newpwfile /tmp/newpass.txt

certutil -S -v 240 -k rsa -n "CN=localhost" -t "u,u,u" -x \
         -s "CN=localhost" -d /tmp/nssdb \
         -f /tmp/newpass.txt -z /tmp/noise.txt

# Workaround for https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1760437
touch /tmp/nssdb/secmod.db

cat <<EOT > conf/pkcs11.cfg
name=testPkcs11
nssLibraryDirectory=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu
nssSecmodDirectory=/tmp/nssdb
nssModule=keystore
EOT
```

Then select the `PKCS11` keystore type in JSignPdf and use `pass123+` as the token PIN.

items.name: JSignPdf_3_0_0-BETA-7
items.body_excerpt:
# Version 3.0.0

Major release with a brand-new desktop UI and modernized Windows
packaging.

## Highlights

- **New JavaFX GUI** — a document-centric desktop experience with PDF
  preview, drag-to-place visible signatures, zoom/page navigation,
  drag-and-drop PDF loading, progress indication, and a collapsible
  side panel for signing options. The legacy Swing UI remains available
  as a fallback via `-Djsignpdf.swing=true`. Encrypted PDFs are now
  handled properly in the preview/signing flow.
- **New Windows installers built with `jpackage`** — replacing the old
  InnoSetup + Launch4j + Ant + Wine toolchain. Each release now ships
  three Windows x64 artifacts: an EXE installer, an MSI installer, and
  a portable ZIP with a bundled JRE. 32-bit Windows is no longer
  supported (Temurin no longer ships 32-bit JDKs); 32-bit and
  Linux/macOS users can still use the cross-arch `jsignpdf-<VERSION>.zip`
  with their own JRE.
- **Java 21 is now the minimum** runtime. The jpackage Windows
  installers and the Flatpak bundle ship their own Java 21 runtime, so
  they are unaffected; users of the platform-independent
  `jsignpdf-<VERSION>.zip` now need a Java 21 (or newer) JRE on `PATH`.
- **Named signature-setting presets** (#360) — save an arbitrary number
  of signing configurations under custom names and switch between them
  from a toolbar combo or the new **Presets** menu (save as new, rename,
  overwrite, delete). If _Store passwords_ is enabled when a preset is
  saved, the preset also captures the signing passwords encrypted with
  the same machine-local seed used for the main config; the encrypted
  values are silently ignored when a preset file is loaded on a
  different user account, and disabling _Store passwords_ before saving
  omits the password keys entirely (recommended before sharing a preset
  file).
- **Configuration has moved** to a platform-appropriate XDG-aware
  location (`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/jsignpdf/` on Linux, `%A

items.body_excerpt:
# Version 3.0.0

Major release with a brand-new desktop UI and modernized Windows
packaging.

## Highlights

- **New JavaFX GUI** — a document-centric desktop experience with PDF
  preview, drag-to-place visible signatures, zoom/page navigation,
  drag-and-drop PDF loading, progress indication, and a collapsible
  side panel for signing options. The legacy Swing UI remains available
  as a fallback via `-Djsignpdf.swing=true`. Encrypted PDFs are now
  handled properly in the preview/signing flow.
- **New Windows installers built with `jpackage`** — replacing the old
  InnoSetup + Launch4j + Ant + Wine toolchain. Each release now ships
  three Windows x64 artifacts: an EXE installer, an MSI installer, and
  a portable ZIP with a bundled JRE. 32-bit Windows is no longer
  supported (Temurin no longer ships 32-bit JDKs); 32-bit and
  Linux/macOS users can still use the cross-arch `jsignpdf-<VERSION>.zip`
  with their own JRE.
- **New Linux Flatpak bundle** — releases now ship a single-file
  `JSignPdf-<VERSION>-linux-x86_64.flatpak` that installs the app under
  the `io.github.intoolswetrust.JSignPdf` ID with a bundled OpenJDK 21
  runtime, a desktop entry, and an icon. Install with
  `flatpak install --user JSignPdf-<VERSION>-linux-x86_64.flatpak` and
  launch via your desktop or `flatpak run io.github.intoolswetrust.JSignPdf`.
- **Java 21 is now the minimum** runtime. The jpackage Windows
  installers and the Flatpak bundle ship their own Java 21 runtime, so
  they are unaffected; users of the platform-independent
  `jsignpdf-<VERSION>.zip` now need a Java 21 (or newer) JRE on `PATH`.
- **Named signature-setting presets** (#360) — save an arbitrary number
  of signing configurations under custom names and switch between them
  from a toolbar combo or the new **Presets** menu (save as new, rename,
  overwrite, delete). If _Store passwords_ is enabled when a preset is
  saved, the preset also captures the signing passwords encrypted with
  the same machine-local seed u

items.name: JSignPdf_3_0_0-BETA-6
items.body_excerpt:
# Version 3.0.0

Major release with a brand-new desktop UI and modernized Windows
packaging.

## Highlights

- **New JavaFX GUI** — a document-centric desktop experience with PDF
  preview, drag-to-place visible signatures, zoom/page navigation,
  drag-and-drop PDF loading, progress indication, and a collapsible
  side panel for signing options. The legacy Swing UI remains available
  as a fallback via `-Djsignpdf.swing=true`. Encrypted PDFs are now
  handled properly in the preview/signing flow.
- **New Windows installers built with `jpackage`** — replacing the old
  InnoSetup + Launch4j + Ant + Wine toolchain. Each release now ships
  three Windows x64 artifacts: an EXE installer, an MSI installer, and
  a portable ZIP with a bundled JRE. 32-bit Windows is no longer
  supported (Temurin no longer ships 32-bit JDKs); 32-bit and
  Linux/macOS users can still use the cross-arch `jsignpdf-<VERSION>.zip`
  with their own JRE.
- **Java 21 is now the minimum** runtime. The jpackage Windows
  installers and the Flatpak bundle ship their own Java 21 runtime, so
  they are unaffected; users of the platform-independent
  `jsignpdf-<VERSION>.zip` now need a Java 21 (or newer) JRE on `PATH`.

## Other changes

- "Reset Settings" action added to the JavaFX UI.
- New and updated translations (Czech, German, French, Spanish, Slovak,
  Portuguese, Croatian, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and
  more — thanks to all Weblate contributors).
- Translation bundles (`messages*.properties`) are now stored in UTF-8,
  simplifying contributions in non-Latin scripts.
- Per-version release notes files are now attached as the GitHub
  Release body.
- User guide restructured around the new JavaFX UI, with a shared
  UI-agnostic "Signing options" reference chapter and a small
  Swing-for-backward-compatibility subsection.
- Improved symlink handling, including macOS (#168).
- Integration tests added for the PDF signing pipeline (#293).
- Moved publishing from OSSRH to Maven Central.
-

[![Top language used](https://img.shields.io/github/languages/top/new92/InstaTools?style=for-the-badge&logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://img.shields.io/github/languages/top/new92/InstaTools?style=for-the-badge&logo=python&logoColor=white) InstaTools offers a suite of powerful automation tools designed specifically for Instagram users. From automating time-consuming tasks to conducting in-depth research, InstaTools streamlines processes that would otherwise demand significant time and effort, particularly for users with large followings. Whether you're managing a high-profile account or conducting market research, these tools provide valuable insights and efficiency. However, it's important to use InstaTools judiciously to avoid risking account suspension or even permanent blocking. Exercise caution and use responsibly to maximize benefits without jeopardizing your account's integrity. > If you find value in this repository, please consider giving it a star and/or forking it. Your support greatly encourages me to create more repositories like InstaTools. If you didn't like it and/or found a bug or you have a suggestion please feel free to contact me at anytime at this email address. Or create a discussion. Or open an issue. Any type of feedback is welcome and appreciated. (Please do not use any vulgar vocabulary) ## Urls 🔗 - [License](https://github.com/new92/InstaTools/blob/main/LICENSE) - [Contributing](https://github.com/new92/InstaTools/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [Code of conduct](https://github.com/new92/InstaTools/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - [You may also find interesting](https://github.com/new92?tab=repositories) ## Author(s) ✍️ - [@new92](https://www.github.com/new92) ## What's new 🚀 - Addition of more tools (Chronos, Delta, Hunter, Sphinx, Spider) - Fixed bugs - Fixed README.md file - Enhanced UI - Added the "save to CSV" option to most tools ## Installation 📥 ### Linux 🐧 ```bash sudo su git clone https://github.com/new92/InstaTools cd InstaTools sudo pip install -r requirements.txt python3 cookies.py cd python3 .py ``` ### Windows 🪟 ```bash git clone https://github.com/new92/InstaTools cd InstaTools pip install -r requirements.txt python3 cookies.py cd python3 .py ``` ### MacOS 🍎 ```bash sudo su git clone https://github.com/new92/InstaTools cd InstaTools python -m pip install -r requirements.txt python3 cookies.py cd python3 .py ``` ## Update 🔄️ ```bash python3 update.py ``` ## Contributing 🤝 Contributions are always welcome! See `contributing.md` for ways to get started. Please adhere to this project's `code of conduct`. For more info please check the `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` file. ## Special thanks to 🫡

## Feedback 💭 If you have any feedback, please reach out to us at this email address. **Feel free to contact us anytime ! You'll get a reply within a day. Please avoid using abusive or offensive language. If you are reporting a bug or making a suggestion please make sure your report/suggestion is as much detailed as possible.** ## Features 🆕 - [ ] New tools - [ ] Enhanced UI - [ ] UX improvements - [ ] Interactive menu mode - [ ] Config support - [ ] Safety & Account protection - [ ] Documentation ## FAQ 🤔 #### Question 1 - Can my account get banned by using InstaTools ? Answer ➡️ Absolutely not. InstaTools strictly adheres to Instagram's guidelines, and it does not violate any privacy-related rules. Therefore, there is no reason for Instagram to block your account. #### Question 2 - Encountering error while attempting to log in ? Answer ➡️ This error can be resolved simply by logging in to your account from your browser, completing Instagram's verification tests and executing the script again. ## License 📜 [![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/new92/IGFollowersIncreaser?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/new92/IGFollowersIncreaser/blob/main/LICENSE) ## Star History [![Star History Chart](https://api.star-history.com/svg?repos=new92/InstaTools&type=Date)](https://star-history.com/#new92/InstaTools&Date)

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: InstaTools offers a suite of powerful automation tools designed specifically for Instagram users. From automating time-consuming tasks to conducting in-depth research, InstaTools streamlines processes that would otherwise demand significant time and effort, particularly for users with large followings. Whether you're managing a high-profile account or conducting market research, these tools provide valuable insights and efficiency. However, it's important to use InstaTools judiciously to avoid risking account suspension or even permanent blocking. Exercise caution and use responsibly to maximize benefits without jeopardizing your account's integrity.

scout.summary: JSignPdf is a mature, cross-platform Java application for digitally signing PDFs with strong support for hardware tokens, long-term validation, and both GUI (JavaFX/Swing) and CLI modes. Recent v3.0.0 betas introduce a modernized UI and Windows packaging but drop 32-bit support.
highlights: Offers comprehensive PDF signing features: PKCS#11/PKCS#12 keystores, RFC 3161 timestamping, OCSP/CRL embedding for LTV, and customizable visible signatures.
Provides dual UI options: modern JavaFX interface with PDF preview and drag-to-place signing, plus legacy Swing fallback.
Distributes platform-specific installers (Windows EXE/MSI with bundled Java 21) and cross-platform ZIP; Flatpak in progress for Linux.
Actively maintained with internationalization via Weblate and Maven artifacts available for embedding the signing engine.
risks: Requires Java 21+, which may hinder adoption in environments stuck on older Java versions.
32-bit Windows support discontinued due to upstream JDK limitations, potentially excluding legacy systems.
macOS and Linux users must manually manage Java 21 dependency unless using future Flatpak.

inventor.summary: JSignPdf is an innovative, cross-platform PDF signing tool that uniquely combines a modern JavaFX desktop UI with drag-to-place visible signatures, comprehensive cryptographic support (PKCS#11/PKCS#12, RFC 3161 timestamping, OCSP/CRL for LTV), and full CLI parity—enabling both interactive and automated workflows.
highlights: Introduces a document-centric JavaFX GUI with real-time PDF preview, drag-to-place visible signatures, and encrypted PDF handling—uncommon in open-source signing tools.
Unifies hardware-backed security (smartcards, HSMs via PKCS#11) with user-friendly desktop interaction and scriptable batch processing in a single application.
Modernizes distribution via jpackage-built Windows installers with bundled Java 21 runtime, eliminating external JRE dependency on Windows.
Enables embedding of its signing engine via Maven Central artifacts (`com.github.kwart.jsign`), allowing third-party integration into custom workflows.
risks: Dependence on Java 21+ limits deployment in legacy enterprise environments still using older JDKs.
Discontinuation of 32-bit Windows support may exclude users on outdated or embedded systems.
macOS and Linux lack native installers with bundled runtimes (pending Flatpak), increasing setup friction.

engineer.summary: JSignPdf is a well-architected Java application that delivers robust PDF digital signing capabilities with strong support for cryptographic standards, modern UI/UX via JavaFX, and flexible deployment options including bundled runtimes for Windows. It maintains backward compatibility through a legacy Swing UI while advancing platform packaging with jpackage.
highlights: Implements comprehensive cryptographic workflows: PKCS#11 (hardware tokens/HSMs), PKCS#12, RFC 3161 timestamping, and OCSP/CRL embedding for LTV compliance.
Features a modern JavaFX GUI with PDF preview, drag-to-place visible signatures, encrypted PDF handling, and full CLI parity—enabling both interactive and automated signing.
Adopts contemporary Java distribution practices: Windows EXE/MSI installers built with jpackage include a bundled Java 21 runtime, eliminating external JRE dependencies on that platform.
Provides Maven Central artifacts (`com.github.kwart.jsign`) for programmatic integration of its signing engine into other Java projects.
risks: Mandates Java 21+, which may block adoption in environments constrained to older JDK versions (e.g., legacy enterprise systems).
Drops 32-bit Windows support due to upstream Temurin JDK limitations, potentially excluding users on older or embedded Windows systems.
macOS and Linux lack native installers with embedded runtimes (Flatpak pending), requiring manual Java 21 setup outside the cross-platform ZIP.

skeptic.summary: JSignPdf presents itself as a robust PDF signing tool, but its recent major overhaul raises concerns about stability, adoption barriers due to Java 21 dependency, and inconsistent platform support—especially given the repeated beta releases with identical changelogs suggesting possible release process issues.
highlights: Despite being labeled as a 'major release', versions 3.0.0-BETA-6, -7, and -8 all contain nearly identical release notes, raising questions about actual progress or whether betas are being recycled without meaningful changes [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
The project mandates Java 21+, a significant barrier in enterprise environments where older JDKs (e.g., Java 8 or 11) remain standard due to compatibility or certification constraints [ID:E-RD-006].
Windows is prioritized with bundled JRE installers, while macOS and Linux users must manually install Java 21—creating an uneven user experience despite claims of cross-platform parity [ID:E-RD-007].

Although Maven artifacts exist for embedding (`com.github.kwart.jsign`), there's no evidence of active third-party adoption or documentation on integration patterns beyond a brief mention [ID:E-RD-008].
risks: Reliance on Java 21+ may severely limit real-world usability in regulated or legacy IT environments that cannot upgrade JVMs easily.
Repeated beta releases with identical content suggest potential instability, lack of feature differentiation, or immature release management.
Dropping 32-bit Windows support without alternative lightweight options could alienate users in resource-constrained or embedded scenarios.
The absence of native installers with bundled runtimes for macOS/Linux (Flatpak still pending) increases setup complexity and reduces accessibility compared to Windows.

scout.summary: InstaTools is a Python-based toolkit offering Instagram automation and scraping capabilities, including tools for follower analysis, data extraction, and account research. It emphasizes user responsibility due to risks of account suspension and includes basic community health files.
highlights: Provides multiple specialized tools (e.g., Poirot, Spider, Hunter) for Instagram automation and reconnaissance.
Includes standard open-source project files: LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, and SECURITY.md.
Actively maintained with recent updates and issue tracking; 291 stars and 31 forks indicate moderate community interest.
Installation and usage instructions are provided via README with clear CLI examples.
risks: Explicitly warns that misuse may lead to Instagram account suspension or blocking [ID:E-RD-003].
Reported issues include failed cookie authentication (HTTP 401) [ID:E-IS-003] and incomplete data scraping (e.g., missing emails/phone numbers) [ID:E-IS-005].
Lack of resume functionality in scraping tasks noted by users [ID:E-IS-006], potentially affecting reliability for large-scale operations.

inventor.summary: InstaTools is an inventive Python-based toolkit that modularizes Instagram automation into specialized, named tools (e.g., Poirot, Spider, Chronos), enabling targeted reconnaissance and data extraction tasks while acknowledging platform policy risks.
highlights: Introduces a suite of creatively named, purpose-built tools (e.g., Hunter for discovery, Poirot for profile scraping, Spider for network mapping) suggesting deliberate architectural design [ID:E-RD-007].
Demonstrates innovation in user workflow with cookie-based authentication via cookies.py and modular script organization per function [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-008].
Expands beyond basic scraping to include research-oriented utilities like Researcher and Tracker, indicating intent for analytical use cases [ID:E-RF-001].
risks: Core functionality relies on Instagram’s private API, making tools fragile to platform changes—as evidenced by HTTP 401 errors on cookie import [ID:E-IS-003].
Incomplete data extraction (e.g., Poirot failing to retrieve emails/phone numbers) suggests limitations in reverse-engineering Instagram’s data exposure [ID:E-IS-005].
Lack of checkpointing or resume capability undermines reliability for long-running inventive tasks [ID:E-IS-006].

engineer.summary: InstaTools is a modular Python toolkit for Instagram automation and data extraction, structured as independent CLI scripts with shared dependencies. It uses cookie-based authentication and provides targeted utilities for reconnaissance, but suffers from reliability issues and lacks robust error handling or state persistence.
highlights: Modular architecture with decoupled tools (e.g., Poirot, Spider, Hunter) organized as separate directories, enabling focused functionality and maintainability [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-007].
Standardized setup via requirements.txt and centralized cookie handling through cookies.py, simplifying initial configuration [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-008].
Includes essential project hygiene files (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md), supporting community contribution and responsible usage [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-006].
risks: Authentication fragility: Cookie import fails with HTTP 401 errors, indicating reliance on unstable or deprecated Instagram API endpoints [ID:E-IS-003].
Incomplete data scraping: Critical fields like emails and phone numbers are not reliably extracted by Poirot, limiting utility for contact discovery [ID:E-IS-005].
No resume or checkpointing mechanism: Long-running operations cannot recover from interruption, reducing reliability for large-scale tasks [ID:E-IS-006].
Potential for account bans due to aggressive automation, as explicitly warned in documentation [ID:E-RD-003].

skeptic.summary: InstaTools presents itself as a powerful Instagram automation toolkit, but its reliability, completeness, and long-term viability are questionable given unresolved technical flaws, incomplete data extraction, and dependence on fragile authentication mechanisms.
highlights: Claims of 'powerful automation tools' [ID:E-RD-003] are undermined by open issues showing core features like Poirot fail to extract basic contact info such as emails and phone numbers [ID:E-IS-005].

The project encourages community engagement and contributions [ID:E-RD-004][ID:E-RD-005], yet provides no clear roadmap or response to known limitations (e.g., lack of resume functionality [ID:E-IS-006]).
Despite modular design and multiple named tools [ID:E-RD-007], there is no evidence of automated testing, CI/CD, or robust error handling beyond basic Ctrl+C exit messaging [ID:E-IS-004].
risks: Authentication via cookies is unreliable—users report HTTP 401 errors during cookie import, suggesting the tool may already be broken against current Instagram API requirements [ID:E-IS-003].
Incomplete scraping capabilities severely limit practical utility; if Poirot cannot retrieve publicly listed contact details, other tools may also underperform [ID:E-IS-005].
No mechanism to resume interrupted operations means large-scale scraping tasks are prone to total failure, wasting time and increasing ban risk without delivering results [ID:E-IS-006].
The README explicitly warns that use may lead to account suspension [ID:E-RD-003], yet offers no mitigation strategies beyond vague 'use responsibly' advice, shifting all liability to the user.
